charts.sql_query:
SELECT *
FROM fact_sales fs, dim_customer dc, dim_product dp, dim_region dr, dim_date dd
WHERE fs.customer_id = dc.customer_id
AND fs.product_id = dp.product_id
AND fs.region_id = dr.region_id
AND fs.date_id = dd.date_id

issues: SELECT * used (1x) — pulls unnecessary columns, increases scan cost
Implicit comma-join style detected (5 tables) — no explicit JOIN conditions, risk of cartesian product
High join count (4 joins) — increases query planning complexity and shuffle cost

decision.recommendation: Chart 'Revenue by Region' has a poorly optimized query (complexity 100/100) even though current cost is moderate. Fixing now prevents cost growth as data volume increases.

charts.sql_query:

WHERE s.warehouse_id = i.warehouse_id
AND s.status != 'DELIVERED'
GROUP BY s.shipment_id

issues: SELECT * used (2x) — pulls unnecessary columns, increases scan cost

GROUP BY combined with SELECT * — expensive aggregation over unnecessary columns

charts.sql_query:
WITH cte1 AS (SELECT * FROM fact_transactions WHERE txn_date >= '2024-01-01'),
cte2 AS (SELECT * FROM fact_transactions WHERE txn_date >= '2024-01-01'),
cte3 AS (SELECT customer_id, SUM(amount) FROM cte1 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT * FROM cte1 c1
JOIN cte2 c2 ON c1.txn_id = c2.txn_id
JOIN cte3 c3 ON c1.customer_id = c3.customer_id

issues: SELECT * used (3x) — pulls unnecessary columns, increases scan cost
Duplicate CTE definitions detected — same computation repeated, wastes compute
GROUP BY combined with SELECT * — expensive aggregation over unnecessary columns

decision.recommendation: Chart 'Customer Churn by Segment' has a poorly optimized query (complexity 80/100) even though current cost is moderate. Fixing now prevents cost growth as data volume increases.
